steps.title: Use the shape of the scatterplot
steps.content: The data rise and then fall, so the model should be a downward-opening parabola. That means the coefficient of $x^2$ must be negative, eliminating choices A and C.

steps.title: Use the intercept size
steps.content: The data values are in the hundreds, and the model should have a positive $y$-intercept around $745$. Choice B has a negative intercept, $-745.73$, so it is not reasonable.

steps.title: Confirm the model
steps.content: Choice D, $$y=-1.674x^2+19.76x+745.73,$$ opens downward and has the correct positive intercept. Therefore choice D is correct.
